summary: PR #2318 matched or improved multiple stage functions across Corneria, Great Bay, Home Run Contest, Mushroom Kingdom/Dream Land 64, Jungle Japes, and Dream Land 64 past-stage files. The diff replaces placeholder markers with C implementations, updates two stage headers with recovered signatures/includes, adds a Home Run GroundVars union arm, and adjusts small stage-local data structs/includes. Bot evidence reports 8 new 100% matches and 6 additional improvements; this partially conflicts with the PR body claim that all listed functions reached fuzzy_match_percent 100.0.

key_files.role: Implemented grCorneria_801E0D30, grCorneria_801E0F6C, and grCorneria_801E2D90 for Great Fox movement, Arwing sequencing, and line/joint standability checks.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists grCorneria_801E0F6C, grCorneria_801E2D90, grCorneria_801E0D30 as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Implemented grGreatBay_801F660C using a stage-local two-column table and loop to align collision vertices with animated JObjs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGb_803E4048 table and grGreatBay_801F660C loop
text_corpus: bot report lists grGreatBay_801F660C as 99.08% improvement, not a new 100% match

key_files.role: Recovered grGreatBay_801F660C prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grGreatBay_801F660C(Ground_GObj*).
Implemented four similar Home Run Contest distance-marker text positioning functions and added grHr_804D6AE4 storage.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grHomeRun_8021DF50, grHomeRun_8021E0D4, grHomeRun_8021E258, grHomeRun_8021E3DC implementations
text_corpus: bot report lists those four functions as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Added lb/forward.h include and recovered grHomeRun_8021EC58 signature as void* grHomeRun_8021EC58(u16).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grhomerun.h include and prototype update

key_files.role: Implemented fn_801FBF6C item callback to find the matching block, assert the stage gobj exists, and respawn/reinitialize the block.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_801FBF6C as 93.72% improvement

key_files.role: Adjusted stage-local config struct from int to s16 fields, added baselib includes, introduced rand_inline helper, and implemented grOldKongo_8021005C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grOk_804D6A90 struct change, baselib/jobj.h and random.h includes, rand_inline, grOldKongo_8021005C
text_corpus: bot report lists grOldKongo_8021005C as 99.76% improvement

key_files.role: Adjusted stage-local config struct from int to s16 fields, added random include, and implemented grOldPupupu_80210C7C random wait selection.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grOp_804D6A98 struct change and grOldPupupu_80210C7C implementation
text_corpus: bot report lists grOldPupupu_80210C7C as new 100% match

key_files.role: Added grHomeRun_GroundVars and a homerun union arm to GroundVars.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct grHomeRun_GroundVars and union arm addition

what_changed: Replaced placeholder markers for stage functions with C implementations in six stage translation units.
Updated grgreatbay.h and grhomerun.h to replace unknown prototypes with recovered signatures.
Added grHomeRun_GroundVars with xC4/xC6/xC8/xCC fields and a Ground union arm for Home Run stage variables.
Converted Old Kongo and Old Pupupu stage-local config structs from a single int field to two s16 fields, matching the random range access patterns in the new implementations.
Added a Great Bay static-style data table grGb_803E4048[][2] and iterated it to update multiple collision vertices from JObj positions.
Added baselib/random.h includes for HSD_Randi usage and baselib/jobj.h in groldkongo.c for JObj operations.
Bot report records 8 new fully matched functions and improvements for fn_801FBF6C, grOldKongo_8021005C, grGreatBay_801F660C, and some data sections.
smart_moves: The Great Bay implementation expresses repeated collision-vertex updates as a table-driven for loop rather than copied blocks, matching the loaded natural-loops standard.
The PR updates owning headers when function bodies recover signatures, aligning with the loaded truthful-headers-and-includes standard.
Adding a Home Run-specific GroundVars union arm in types.h is a typed-field recovery step rather than relying only on raw pointer arithmetic, though several accesses still use gv.unk in the diff.
The Corneria line check keeps the unknown function name but uses local semantic variables such as line_id, joint, and result where supported by calls to stage_info and mpJointFromLine.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated stage geometry updates can often be represented as a small index table plus a natural loop, as shown by grGreatBay_801F660C mapping JObj indices to collision vertex IDs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGb_803E4048 table and for (i = 0; i < 7; i++) loop

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched body proves a stage callback/helper signature, update the stage header instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Stage-local data struct widths may be inferred from access patterns in matching functions; this PR changed Old Kongo and Old Pupupu config fields from int to s16 before using them as random range endpoints.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grOk_804D6A90 and grOp_804D6A98 struct changes from int x0 to s16 x0/s16 x2
raw_diff: HSD_Randi range expressions using x0 and x2

decomp_lessons.lesson: A PR body's verification summary should be cross-checked against bot reports; here the body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, while the bot reports only 8 new 100% matches and several partial improvements.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification says fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists fn_801FBF6C at 93.72%, grOldKongo_8021005C at 99.76%, grGreatBay_801F660C at 99.08%

naming_conventions.convention: Stage functions retain address-style names such as grCorneria_801E0D30, grGreatBay_801F660C, and fn_801FBF6C when stronger semantic names are not established.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implemented functions preserve address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Common local role names are used where roles are clear: gobj/Ground_GObj, gp/Ground*, ip/Item*, jobj/HSD_JObj*, pos/Vec3, text/HSD_Text*.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local declarations across grcorneria.c, grgreatbay.c, grhomerun.c, grinishie1.c

naming_conventions.convention: New Home Run ground variable fields remain offset-style xC4/xC6/xC8/xCC rather than speculative semantic names.
Stage-local data symbols keep address-style names such as grGb_803E4048, grHr_804D6AE4, grOk_804D6A90, and grOp_804D6A98.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: stage-local symbols in grgreatbay.c, grhomerun.c, groldkongo.c, groldpupupu.c

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used HSD_ASSERT(1398, jobj) in grGreatBay_801F660C after Ground_801C3FA4 lookup and before lb_8000B1CC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Consistent with assert macro cleanup, but no review discussion explains line-number derivation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(16) at the end of fn_801FBF6C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The loaded evidence lacks narrow objdiff rationale for PAD_STACK; bot report says this function only improved to 93.72%, so this should not be treated as a generally accepted tactic from this PR alone.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used repeated Ground_801C0498 calls and explicit 0.0F / -1.0F expressions in four Home Run text-position functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Evidence supports that these expressions appeared in the matching implementations; no broader style rule should be inferred.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a small static inline rand_inline helper in Old Kongo while Old Pupupu inlined equivalent ternary/random-range logic directly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: groldkongo.c rand_inline and groldpupupu.c ternary HSD_Randi expression
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Only local evidence; do not generalize helper-vs-inline style without sibling checks.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a casted halfword store *(s16*) &gp->gv.unk.xC4 in grOldPupupu_80210C7C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is a raw/casted access and may indicate unrecovered GroundVars typing; route as follow-up rather than accepted style.

review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 shows matched code 58.78% (+0.04%, +1440 bytes), 8 new matches, and 6 improvements in unmatched items/data sections.
No review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files and exact code edits from raw_diff and changed_files.jsonl.

Verification evidence is mixed: the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the bot report lists only 8 new 100% matches and records fn_801FBF6C, grOldKongo_8021005C, and grGreatBay_801F660C as partial improvements.
The PR body lists fn_8021E994 in grhomerun.c, but the loaded diff excerpt/full raw_diff evidence does not show an implementation for fn_8021E994; do not claim it changed beyond the body mention.
No human review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale cannot be inferred.
Some tactics such as PAD_STACK and casted halfword store are visible but lack explicit objdiff rationale in the loaded slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use table-driven natural loops for repeated stage collision/JObj updates when the mapping is explicit
accepted_candidate_records.text: grGreatBay_801F660C used grGb_803E4048[][2] and a for loop over seven entries to map JObj positions to collision vertex updates with mpVtxSetPos. This is a PR-supported example of replacing repeated geometry-update logic with a compact natural loop.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should be recovered with matched stage bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR updated grGreatBay_801F660C and grHomeRun_8021EC58 declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures alongside the body work.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreatbay.h and grhomerun.h prototype changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not treat PR body fuzzy-match claims as sole verifier evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2318 body says all listed functions reached fuzzy_match_percent 100.0, while the bot report distinguishes 8 new 100% matches from partial improvements. Intake should preserve that discrepancy.

source_update_candidates.title: Stage matching work in PR #2318 touched multiple gr stage translation units and headers
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2318 changed src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c, grgreatbay.c/.h, grhomerun.c/.h, grinishie1.c, groldkongo.c, groldpupupu.c, and types.h for stage-function matching and type/header recovery.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help path-scoped search for historical stage matching examples.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit rationale for PAD_STACK in partially matched functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2318 includes PAD_STACK(16) in fn_801FBF6C, while bot evidence reports the function as an improvement to 93.72% rather than a full match. Consider emphasizing that PAD_STACK should not be treated as accepted style without narrow verifier evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standard already covers this, but this PR is a useful cautionary example.

source_update_candidates.title: Home Run Contest stage variables gained a dedicated GroundVars union arm in PR #2318
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2318 added struct grHomeRun_GroundVars with xC4, xC6, xC8, xCC and added a homerun arm to GroundVars, used by grhomerun.c functions around distance-marker text setup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct grHomeRun_GroundVars and union arm
raw_diff: grHomeRun_8021DF50 and sibling functions use gp->gv.homerun.xC6
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful as a path fact for future Home Run GroundVars cleanup; curator should verify current source before accepting.

rejection_notes: Do not record that all 11 listed functions fully matched; bot evidence contradicts the PR body for at least grGreatBay_801F660C, grOldKongo_8021005C, and fn_801FBF6C.
Do not claim fn_8021E994 was implemented in this PR from the loaded diff; it is only mentioned in the PR body and absent from the loaded diff evidence.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(16), casted halfword stores into gv.unk, or repeated Ground_801C0498/0.0F expressions as accepted general style; the PR evidence only shows local use.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale because no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
